Transcrição Completa: 20 Veo3 AI Ad Creation - Hosted By Timothy Schucker 8 9 25

 

 

[00:00] Cool. So, for all those who don't know, V3 is a tool from Google. They released it with their big old Gemini update that they did, I think, in the most recent like three or four months.

 

[00:15] Um, it's pretty cool. There's a lot of great stuff that you can do with it, ranging from using it for the initial hook of the ad and then just using a standard body and CTA that you may have filmed after that. It's good for hook variation.

 

[00:30] Um, also just to be clear, there's companies as large as like the Hard Rock that is using it for their complete advertising efforts at this point. As an example, living here in Miami, I get targeted by the uh Florida Hard Rock division literally every day with ads to go and do sports gambling and they are 100% VO3 ads.

 

[00:52] Um, interestingly enough though, I will point out that with every Hard Rock ad that I see, there's a good like several dozen comments of regular people saying they're going to boycott the Hard Rock for using AI ads.

 

[01:05] So, I want to be fair and saying there's some controversy with what you're going to see in comments potentially when you run these kind of things. But, it's also quite the opposite, too. There's also people depending on how good of an ad you create that absolutely love these things and it can skyrocket the performance of ad campaigns comparatively.

 

[01:25] So moral of the story whether you're looking to make literal full-blown movies like what Euan has in as as an example and run them as ads or whether you're looking to just make you know shorter more attention grabbing ads for your specific industry and niche. We'll learn a lot here today.

 

[01:42] Timothy specifically in the inner circle here has opted and volunteered to give us a class today on how he's using V3. Um it's actually pretty interesting. He shared a few of his videos uh ranging from people skydiving to it's it's reason it's interesting.

 

[01:58] I want to be clear when I say this. He's in a niche that you could consider like rather boring to a degree. you know, it's essentially him selling to insurance agents who are selling things like ILS, uh, term life policies, whole life insurance policies, just they're selling insurance in general.

 

[02:18] So, moral of the story is in an industry like that, rather than just shooting like basic videos of yourself, you can use all this crazy stuff that V3 can allow you to do comparatively. So, there's a lot of cool creative stuff you can do.

 

[02:32] A lot of people struggle with things like character consistency throughout VO3. Sometimes you'll come across like credit issues. There's third party softwares that you can use. U long story short, there's going to be a tremendous amount that we all learn here today.

 

[02:47] Um I'd consider most of us relatively new to this. I'm sure to be fair to most of you guys. You guys have messed around with it a little bit, but not all of you have actually committed to running ads with it. So that's what we're here to learn about today.

 

[03:02] Without further ado, we'll give it up to Timothy and let him take away the uh the class for today. Go ahead.

 

[03:08] Cool. Cool. What's going on everyone?

 

[03:11] What up?

 

[03:12] Um, let me share my screen here. And I'll just give you guys basically kind of a brief background. Um, say like maybe two months ago, Nick and I had tried to do some stuff on VO3. Just found it to be extremely time consuming. We were like typing all the prompts inside of V3.

 

[03:30] basically kind of just like gave up on it for a while until I had a conversation with um actually Cole Gordon's CMO and he was telling me that they were using it uh basically as like a UGC replacement.

 

[03:42] So, I'm going to show you guys like a couple different use cases, ways that we've used it. I'll kind of tell you guys about our experience and then what I think would be super valuable and helpful is kind of just like building out an ad live here.

 

[03:55] So, if anyone has um like a script that of one of their proven ads that you guys want to share, we can basically take that and then turn it into an ad.

 

[04:05] So, our I'll start off by showing you guys kind of a couple examples of our ads. Let me uh share the sound so you guys can see this. Okay, cool.

 

[04:16] So basically what we did is we took ads that we had already found worked with me in them and then we essentially just replaced me with a hot AI Latino woman and the ads ended up outperforming the ones with me surprise surprise.

 

[04:32] So I'll play the first example and then I'll show you guys the other.

[04:38] [AD PLAYBACK] If you're selling IL annuity or cash value whole life insurance and you want to sell to high networth clients, you need to be seen as a trusted expert, not a telemarketer.

 

[04:52] If your clients don't view you as an expert, they're not going to trust you with their money. The truth is, your sales skills aren't the problem. It's your brand.

 

[05:03] You see, there's top producers in this space that aren't good at sales, but their brand positions them as an expert. That way, wealthy clients book appointments on your calendar, already trusting you and wanting to buy.

 

[05:17] Our clients are closing five and six figure policies every single week using this exact strategy. And our company's CEO, Tim, is hosting a free live training to show you how you can start positioning yourself as an expert using AI and branding.

 

[05:32] So, go ahead and click that link down below to register for the free training. Or you can just sit around and let this opportunity pass you by while other agents get rich. Totally up to you.

[05:52] An hour.

 

[05:54] Yeah, like an hour and a half basically. I was I was hoping someone was going to say like five hours, but Jeremy was pretty on point. Yeah, it took like an hour and a half to make that.

 

[06:05] And like, you know, I would argue that that ad is probably it looks it's more engaging than like an Harmon Brothers ad where you would spend 500 grand to create it and it would take like probably six months to even produce something and you can literally spit these out in like an hour.

NÚMEROS: CTR: 0.98% → 2.5% | CPL: $25-30 → $6-7 | Cost per booked call: $250 → $67

 

VISUAL: Comparação de métricas antes e depois, setas indicando melhoria dramática

 

[08:28] Yeah. So, our cost per lead um and this was like to have we were doing like conversion leads with an instant form to test out a ton of ads. It was like around like 25-30 bucks. With these, we were consistently getting leads for like six or seven bucks.

 

[08:45] So, dramatically decreased our cost. Our cost per book call went from like 250 on an average campaign to we were getting we had one of these adsets that was ripping like a $67 cost per book call.

 

[09:00] Um and there was a little bit of an issue with the tracking. So we don't know the exact number of sales that have come from this. But we've gotten extremely positive feedback from our clients especially because we're a marketing agency.

 

[09:13] And so it's like it's almost like a meta concept where because we're doing we have really good ads, they're like, "Oh, these guys obviously know how to make really good ads."

 

[09:23] So, um, it's not like we've been doing this for like six months in full transparency. So, like I don't have six months of data to report you guys. But what I can tell you is like from the early results of what we've seen, like we're going all in on VO3

 

[09:38] and basically, you know, these ads take less time to film than like ripping a ton of variations. And then the other thing too is that they're modular, right?

 

[09:48] So essentially what you do whenever you create these is you get like a ton of 8-second clips. Like I probably have a hundred little random 8-second clips on my computer.

 

[10:00] So, kind of like what Jeremy was talking about a couple weeks ago with like creative testing is I can very easily create infinite variations of these ads just by assigning tasks to my video editors.

 

[10:13] Like I don't got to go out and film more stuff and I can have the craziest things happen in the scene. So, I could have the hook of her um doing a bank robbery. I I made another one where she's like scaling a mountain full of money. Like the mountain's made out of cash.

 

[10:30] I could do another one where she's just talking at a pool. Like anything you can possibly imagine, you can create with these ads and you can test infinite numbers of like number of avatars and people.

 

[10:42] So if you guys thought at the end, I use the same ending, the same CTA for both of those ads. So I could just clip that in at anyone and I can split test very easily and create it and do a lot more than I can do as uh an individual. Right?

[11:15] I don't know if he's on this call, but what he did was he basically had the VO3 as the hook intro him in his app. So, that's like super easy to do. You could probably do that in like 30 seconds and test tons of variations of that.

 

[11:30] So, you can basically kind of have like the VO3 tie in and intro you in the ad. That's one way to do it. Or you can do it kind of the way that we did where we just took a proven script and made variations of it. I'll show you guys how to do both.

 

[11:45] Um, but who here has ever seen like Vshreed's ads? You guys have probably seen those where it's like it's basically just like brain rot stacked like stacked on top of each other. uh it just like repeats itself over and over, but it's super engaging.

 

[12:00] And so it'll be like some woman in a bikini is like, "Have you seen this guy all over your social media?" And then it goes over to uh the Vshreed guy and then it's just a bunch of ads clipped together.

 

[12:12] So I think you could do something like that with VO3 and just basically replace UGC. So that's another way that I've seen people use it that uh seems to work.

 

[12:22] But the main thing that we've done is the uh variation of a proven concept and just taking something where it's like me direct to camera and just spicing it up and making it more engaging.

 

[12:33] Uh and then also very easy to make tons and tons and tons of hook variations, CTA variations, body variations, everything.

[16:00] And so this one V3 quality is what it defaults to, I'm pretty sure. Or maybe I just selected this and I don't know, but this one uses 100 credits per time you generate. And half the time the videos suck.

 

[16:14] So V3 fast is actually faster and in my opinion it's just as good and it's like literally a tenth of the price. It's $10 per uh generation you do.

[26:20] And you could tell whatever your preferred LLM is, whether it's ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, etc. to make an extremely detailed JSON file of that character. So like the features of the character, how they look. That way you can ensure there's a higher probability of character consistency.

[28:08] And then pro tip, you you can't use actual people in uh VO3. So, you have to also describe it to be like a striking lookalike. It's like one of those hypothetical ones where you get it to commit a crime without actually getting it to commit a crime because you say it's hypothetical.

 

[28:25] Okay, cool. We just we'll just keep it simple with this first one, honestly. Um, cool. So, let me remove the frame to video. We'll go back to text to video and then boom, boom.

 

[28:38] And then literally this is just going to create. And that's I mean, guys, that's pretty much the process is I'm just essentially just going back and forth from the script. As these videos are generating, I'm going ahead and creating the next prompt.

 

[28:53] and then adding the next thing in the script. And honestly, like it's it's performing pretty well today. Maybe maybe uh maybe they made some improvements to it like over the weekend or something or throughout the end of the week. I don't know.

 

[29:08] But uh yeah, this is like this is this was performing better than it normally does to be honest. So I'm pretty happy about it. But yeah, you guys have questions about specific process. Yeah, go ahead.

 

[29:22] Yeah. So you just gave it two different prompts. So you you gave it like the first one and then you gave it like the Joe Rogan lookalike one. [29:38] Actually, I fed it the same prompt just twice.

 

[29:42] You fed it the same prompt but twice.

 

[29:44] Yeah. The reason I do that is like because most of the time like half the half of them will be terrible. Um and so if I do four I don't have to like you see how it takes a minute to load.

 

[29:57] It doesn't, from what I found, it doesn't take longer if you do multiple at once. But if I just wait for it to be good, then I'm delaying my process of getting this done. So like you can see right here,

[34:47] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Retargeting Strategy - Hammer Them Ads

 

NÚMEROS: 3-5 second videos, 5-6 ad impressions

 

VISUAL: Estratégia de retargeting com fotos convertidas em GIFs, screenshots de reviews

 

[34:48] We basically took a still photo and then turned it into like a GIF. Um, it's like a three to five second video. So, we can do the same hammer them thing with like a Trust Pilot review or a screenshot of a client saying something good about us.

 

[35:05] And so, for me, like whenever I go on Instagram, I'll just see like tons of screenshots of our clients saying good things about us. I'll see my ads with my face in it. And then I'll also see the VO3.

 

[35:18] So, it's just like we're hitting them from all these different angles where it's like you just can't get away. Like you're going to get you're like you're going to have probably five to six impressions of ads, but it's hard to get ad blindness because it just looks different.

 

[35:33] Um, that's where I think like really the power of this would be is like combining VO3 of like different hooks and different UGC creators introing you where you could have like an old person, you could have a young person, you could have like a a guy, you could have a girl,

 

[35:50] like different, you know, races, ethnicities of of people like just tons of creative variation is like super easy to do. And I think honestly it could replace UGC because UGC creators like suck to deal with nowadays.

 

[36:05] I would say um one other thing about the uh quality of leads to speak to here. Um as far as the quality goes like we have had sales uh popped through our V3 ads as recent as they are like uh I'm not exactly sure like you are as well because of our recent tracking issues.

 

[36:25] But we have had and like even with our longer sales cycle, we have had several of these pop very early and some of them even through uh webinar ads and our cost per lead overall is cut in half on average across all of the V3 ads relative to the ads with you in it.

 

[36:45] And uh along with that the cost per call

 

[36:48] Yeah, the cost per call is uh down significantly. It's down about $100 like around like a 250 average is what we were running before. like it was like 220 to 250 and now we're down on average of the V3 ads to around like 140 150.

[39:22] because like they're probably seeing multiple of them. And so it's like, you know, they might have clicked on this one, but the one that actually convinced them was a different one. Or it was just the overall frequency of seeing it a ton that actually sold them and convinced them.

 

[39:38] So I think that that kind of stuff is useful, but I don't put too much stock in it personally just because I feel like you kind of get into a trap where like you just only focus on like the ones that are directly converting when the other ones actually can play a factor as well. [40:37] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Post-Production Workflow

 

NÚMEROS: 5 minutes editing time

 

VISUAL: iMovie timeline com clipes, menção a VEED.io para legendas

 

[40:38] Yeah. Uh 100%. One sec. V3 SOP.

 

[40:45] Yeah. So what I did is I kind of just like I when I first got it, I just wanted to see what the finished product looked like. Normally, I would have sent it to a video editor and had them do do everything, but like here, I have all my clips in iMovie.

 

[41:00] So, I mean, it'll take me like five minutes to go through and just like chop this up and make it look nice, right? I don't know why it's whacking out, but um anyway, you kind of just like do what you normally would with editing, making them engaging and and cut fast.

 

[41:17] And then I just added music over them. I found this. There's like this song that is in the video editor VEED.IO, which is what I use to do the the captions. Um, so I just downloaded that song. I add it in on iMovie and then I'll just upload it into VEED.IO and I'll have it automatically do the transcriptions.

 

[41:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Organic Post Style Ad Format

[41:55] which I think is a that's a pretty cool way of doing it because it almost is a little more sneaky where it doesn't look like an ad like testing this kind of thing as a banner and then we even did it where it's like our own company sharing the video.

 

[42:10] And these are performing really well for us, too. But you can make this in Canva in just a few minutes. [42:28] And then it's just like the captions. But like, you know, if I'm in an ad talking direct to camera, usually I'm going to have the video editors fill like some graphics on it and like popups, animations, and things like that. So, it takes a little longer.

 

[42:43] But with this, like because the scenes are so engaging anyway, you can just like you it doesn't really need much editing. If you overedited these videos, if anything, I think it would take away from it. Um, but yeah, that's pretty much the process I've been doing.

 

[43:00] Yeah, I can show you with you guys like Yeah. Yeah, exactly. It's like it's more sneaky. Um, but also I think uh you and with some of the stuff that you've done like with your ads like depending on the ones that I've seen is I think the more like the it's like with anything

 

[43:20] the more you go towards entertainment and it's not still hitting like the main angle of targeting the people you want to target. I think that's where people are probably getting lead quality issues because the videos are so interesting that like anyone will watch them.

 

[43:37] Like you guys have seen Euan's ad with the PDF method, like that ad's fire. I'm probably never going to buy the PDF method course. Um, but I would watch that whole ad like 10 times.

 

[43:50] And so I think it's like if you can just take an ad that you already have that's like targeting the right people and then just make it more interesting, I think that's a pretty it's a pretty safe use case for it.

 

[44:05] And it doesn't take that much effort because you're just like you're literally just talking into your phone or your computer and having it spit everything out for you.

[45:42] I'll just add something quickly. So, um, on the whole character consistency thing, one f one thing that I found works really well, I know I put a note in the in the telegram, but it might have got better the other day when everyone was going crazy messaging about two or three.

 

[46:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Character Consistency Hack

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Processo: Gerar imagens primeiro → Usar Runway para consistência → Frame to video no Veo3

 

[46:02] Um, but one thing I found works well is generating images of each scene first. So you can use something like free pick for that and then use the frames to video because with the image generation you can use something um called runway

 

[46:20] which essentially means you can have the exact same character in totally different settings and then you generate the images of that throw those images into V3 and then from there it's like it's the exact same character even holding specific products or literally any any scene you want.

 

[46:38] It can be the same character doing it that way and then you can obviously just add voice. But yeah, there's there's so many different ways to to do it, but that's the way I found if you want like consistent characters doing it from images and then using that frames to video.

 

[46:55] That's fire. Yeah, I think it's I think we're like in the early stages of VO3, like kind of like what UGC was like back in the day. And I think like I think we all have like a crazy window. If you capitalize on it, like I think you can get some some really good results. before everyone else in in your industry catches on.

 

[47:18] Does VL3 do 16 by 9 or is it always like landscape style?

 

[47:24] I haven't gotten it to do 16 by 9. Um, I guess you could probably prompt it to just have the character standing like in the middle and then just have your editors clip it.

 

[47:35] Um, you have you seen anything different? I haven't I haven't

 

[47:40] What I've usually found is if you want something like super important, for example, you want that specific dimension or anything that's really really important to include in the video, I found that if you include that at the start of the prompt, then it cuts more weight towards that.

 

[47:58] So, if you was to put the start of the prompt, this video needs to be 16 by 9. It will be hit or miss, but generally you will find that like Timothy there is generating four videos per generation, right? One of those will come out 16 by 9.

 

[48:15] But yeah, I've not really found a way to get it 100% of the time, but there is ways to do it and that's like the number one way I found.

 

[48:24] Okay, cool. Thank you.

 

[48:27] One other thing I will add, this is not necessarily like tied into VO3, but just another way we've been using AI is like basically instead of creating like SOPs within our company, we'll just create a GPT for the team members to use which requires left thinking.

 

[48:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: AI Workflow Automation - Custom GPTs

 

NÚMEROS: 45 second monologue → full task breakdown

 

VISUAL: GPTs customizados: SOP Generator, Task Assignment Generator

 

[48:47] So like um we literally made a we made a GPT that creates SOPs in like a specific format. So someone can just film a Loom, copy the Loom transcript, paste it into the SOP generator, and it builds the whole SOP for them because like people are lazy. Like I hate building SOPs.

 

[49:08] So I actually made it more for myself. But like for everyone on our team now, there's no excuse to not document the processes of what you're doing. Like same thing with assigning tasks. Like I literally just I made it where it spits it out in a consistent format.

 

[49:25] So I can just like voice chat into my phone. Hey, this is what I want this person to do. And I can just have like a 45 second monologue and then it'll assign it into a specific task with like step-by-step instructions and everything written out.

 

[49:40] And so like any process that you have in your company, you can probably create a GPT that can do a lot of it for you and then you can just give your employees that GPT and teach them how to use it. And so that's been something we've been implementing that's been huge for us.

 

[49:57] The SOP generator. Yes. Yeah. I have it's like in our company's internal
